free_text: Spin assignment and statistical properties of neutron
resonances from 161,163Dy(n,gamma) and 167Er(n,gamma)
measured at the DANCE facility
The DANCE detector consists of 160 BaF2
scintillation crystals forming an array with a solid
angle of 3.5pi. The detection efficiency for a 1-MeV
photon is 86% and the energy resolutions for 1- and
6-MeV photons are about 16 and 7%, respectively. To
reduce the number of neutrons scattered from the
sample, a 6-cm-thick 6LiH shell is placed between the
sample and the crystals.
All the samples in the form of self-supporting metal
foils were prepared at the Oak Ridge National
Laboratory.
Resonance properties were deduced from the measured
gamma-ray multiplicities.
Approved by A.Couture on August 22, 2024.
BP
Rough estimates based on the observed position of
maxima. Authors analyzed spectra up to neutron energy
of 400 eV; above 209 eV, only resonances with firmly
or tentatively assigned spin are reported.

Rough estimates based on the observed position of
maxima. All resonance structures were analyzed up to
energy 285 eV, and firm and tentative spin
assignments are presented up to 670 eV.
Tentative assignment.
No information on uncertainties.
Authors determined average resonance spacing for both
pure spin for lower and higher spins, and mixed-spin
sequences assuming that the probability distribution
of the number of subthreshold resonances due to
Porter-Thomas fluctuations is known.
the optimum statistic proposed by Dyson
and Mehta allows spacing determination with the
relative uncertainty of 4/(SQRT(2PI))/N for pure and
4/pi/N ~ 1.27/N for mixed-spin sequences of two spins
if the level positions follow the predictions of GOE.
In practice, the uncertainties based on experimental
data must be larger due to uncertainty related to the
sequence completeness.
